messages.content:
1. Introduction
Graphene, as a single layer two-dimensional material, has attracted increased interest in catalysis, hydrogen storage, and many other ﬁelds [1–4]. For heterogeneous single-atom catalysts, graphene has been reported to be a superb support. Graphene has also been considered as a nano-reinforcement for composites [5–7]. The interfacial properties of graphene are signiﬁcant [8], and considerable research on the adsorption process [9–12] has been carried out. The hitherto-investigated elements used in metal single-atom supported by graphene include over half of the metal elements (37/64) in the ﬁrst to sixth periods [2].
Due to the huge number of combinations of adatoms and adsorption sites, which include the top (T) site, the bridge (B) site and the hollow (H) site, computational methods are widely used in accelerating possible combination selection in order to save precious time and resources.
The most common of these many simulation methods is a ﬁrst-principle calculation based on density functional theory (DFT) [13–16], because of its advantages in assessing the chemical and mechanical stability of novel materials. However, the computing cost of a DFT calculation is huge. In order to balance the need for calculation accuracy and the computing cost, we employed the machine learning (ML) method to accelerate the DFT calculation.

ML is a widely used data-driven statistics approach that balances the computing cost and calculation accuracy well in the development of novel materials. In order to enhance the possibility of establishing accurate predictive rules, many attempts have been performed with small datasets, which are typically used in materials research [17–19]. The advances in applying ML to scientiﬁc research provide new opportunities for balancing calculating accuracy and costs [20–22]. This is because the ML workﬂow bypasses the computationally costly step of solving the Schrödinger equation [23].
A typical workﬂow for accelerating a DFT calculation with ML consists of three necessary steps. First, the atomic structure must be transformed into a numerical descriptor.
The descriptor usually contains both atomic and location information for each atom. This descriptor is then used as an input for an ML model. After training the ML model, the properties prediction is provided as the ﬁnal step of the workﬂow. Compared with solving the Schrödinger equation, the computational speed of the ML process is faster, by several orders of magnitude. 请翻译成中文

1. 引言
石墨烯作为一种单层二维材料，在催化、氢储存等领域引起了越来越多的关注[1-4]。对于异相单原子催化剂来说，石墨烯被报道为一种出色的支撑材料。石墨烯还被认为是复合材料的纳米增强材料[5-7]。石墨烯的界面性质具有重要意义[8]，并且对吸附过程进行了大量研究[9-12]。迄今为止，在由石墨烯支持的金属单原子中研究的元素包括前6个周期中的超过一半金属元素（37/64）[2]。

由于原子和吸附位点的巨大组合数量，包括顶位（T位）、桥位（B位）和空位（H位），计算方法被广泛应用于加速可能的组合选择，以节省宝贵的时间和资源。

其中一个最常见的模拟方法是基于密度泛函理论（DFT）的第一原理计算[13-16]，因为它在评估新材料的化学和力学稳定性方面具有优势。然而，DFT计算的计算成本很高。为了平衡计算精度和计算成本的需求，我们采用了机器学习（ML）方法来加速DFT计算。

ML是一种广泛使用的数据驱动统计方法，在新材料的开发中很好地平衡了计算成本和计算精度。为了增加建立准确预测规则的可能性，许多尝试使用通常在材料研究中使用的小数据集进行了尝试[17-19]。将ML应用于科学研究的进展为平衡计算精度和成本提供了新的机会[20-22]。这是因为ML工作流程绕过了求解薛定谔方程的计算昂贵步骤[23]。

使用ML加速DFT计算的典型工作流程包括三个必要步骤。首先，必须将原子结构转换为数值描述符。描述符通常包含每个原子的原子和位置信息。然后，将该描述符作为ML模型的输入。经过训练后，预测属性作为工作流程的最终步骤提供。与求解薛定谔方程相比，ML过程的计算速度更快，快几个数量级。
Adsorption and modification behavior of single atoms on the surface of single vacancy graphene: Machine learning accelerated first principle computations 翻译成中文
石墨烯单空位表面上单原子的吸附和修饰行为：机器学习加速的第一原理计算
2.1.Crystal structure and calculations method  翻译成中文
2.2.Machine learning databases and models翻译成中文
2.2 机器学习数据库和模型
Results and discussion  翻译成中文

4. Conclusions
In this paper, we investigate the adsorption behavior of single atoms adsorbed on single vacancy graphene using machine learning- accelerated first-principles calculations. We calculate the adsorption energy and distance using density functional theory of first princi- ples to build database. Characteristic parameters, such as atomic and ionic radii, were utilized to construct a dataset for machine learning purposes. Machine learning models, including BPNN, KNN, SVM, and DT, were used to construct mathematical models. Our analysis showed that the BPNN model was the most suitable for the dataset, based on decision coefficients and root mean square error. We further improved the BPNN model using genetic algorithms to enhance the coefficient of determination while reducing the root mean square error of the model. The GA-BPNN model was used to predict the full periodic table of atoms, and we selected atoms from these predictions that were capable of modifying graphene. To verify the accuracy of the predictions, we employed first-principles calculations of the elemen- tal modification of single vacancy graphene. The phonon spectrum calculation verified the thermodynamic stability of the system. The use of machine learning to accelerate the first-principles approach shortened the development time and accelerated the research process of elemental modification of graphene. The investigation of the adsorption behavior of single atoms from the full periodic table on the surface of single vacancy graphene offers a comprehensive understanding to experimentally modify graphene.  翻译成中文

4. 结论
在本文中，我们利用机器学习加速的第一原理计算研究了单个原子吸附在单个空位石墨烯上的吸附行为。我们使用基于第一原则的密度泛函理论计算吸附能量和距离，构建了数据库。我们利用原子和离子半径等特征参数构建了用于机器学习的数据集。我们使用了BPNN、KNN、SVM和DT等机器学习模型构建了数学模型。我们的分析结果显示，BPNN模型是最适合该数据集的模型，基于决定系数和均方根误差。我们进一步使用遗传算法改进了BPNN模型，提高了模型的决定系数，并降低了均方根误差。我们使用GA-BPNN模型预测了周期表中的所有原子，并从这些预测中选择出能够修饰石墨烯的原子。为了验证预测的准确性，我们使用第一原理计算了单个空位石墨烯的元素修饰。声子频谱计算验证了系统的热力学稳定性。利用机器学习加速第一原理方法，缩短了石墨烯元素修饰的开发时间，并加速了研究过程。对周期表中所有原子在单个空位石墨烯表面的吸附行为的研究为实验上的石墨烯修饰提供了全面的理解。

Declaration of competing interest

This research is supported by the science foundation of national key laboratory of science and technology, China on advanced composites in special environments.翻译成中文

致谢
本研究得到中国国家重点实验室科技部的支持，该实验室研究了特殊环境下高级复合材料的科学基础。
